questions.question: When did India become an independent nation?
questions.explanation: India gained independence from British rule on 15 August 1947 after a long freedom struggle.

questions.question: What was the Constituent Assembly responsible for?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly prepared the Constitution to establish rules and principles for governing the country.

questions.question: When did the Constitution of India come into effect?
questions.explanation: On this day India became a sovereign democratic republic under the Constitution.

questions.question: Why were Indian states reorganized in 1956?
questions.correct_answer: To form states mainly based on language
questions.explanation: The States Reorganisation Act reorganized state boundaries according to linguistic and cultural identities.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the Five-Year Plans?
questions.correct_answer: To guide economic development and national growth
questions.explanation: Five-Year Plans focused on improving agriculture, industry, infrastructure, and living standards.

questions.question: What was the Green Revolution?
questions.correct_answer: An agricultural movement that increased food production using modern farming techniques
questions.explanation: High-yield seeds, fertilizers, and irrigation helped increase wheat and rice production in India.

questions.question: Why is India considered a multilingual country?
questions.correct_answer: Because many different languages are spoken across the country
questions.explanation: India has numerous regional languages, and government policies respect this linguistic diversity.

questions.question: Why did cities grow rapidly after independence?
questions.correct_answer: Because industrial development created more job opportunities
questions.explanation: People migrated from rural areas to cities in search of employment and better facilities.

questions.question: How did science and technology contribute to India's development?
questions.correct_answer: By establishing research institutions and advancing space and scientific programs
questions.explanation: Organizations and research institutions helped India progress in communication, space exploration, and technology.

questions.question: What is national integration?
questions.correct_answer: The unity of people from different cultures, languages, and regions within a nation
questions.explanation: Despite diversity, democratic institutions and policies help maintain unity in India.

notes.title: Independent India
notes.content: India became independent in 1947 and faced challenges such as partition, refugee rehabilitation, and nation-building.

notes.title: Indian Constitution
notes.content: The Constitution provides the framework for governance and ensures rights, equality, and justice for citizens.

notes.title: States Reorganisation
notes.content: In 1956, Indian states were reorganized mainly on linguistic lines to improve administration and representation.

notes.title: Economic Planning
notes.content: The government introduced Five-Year Plans to guide development in agriculture, industry, and infrastructure.

notes.title: Agricultural Development
notes.content: The Green Revolution helped India increase food production and become more self-sufficient in food grains.

facts.fact: India became independent from British rule on 15 August 1947.
The Constitution of India came into effect on 26 January 1950.
The States Reorganisation Act of 1956 reorganized states mainly on linguistic lines.
The Green Revolution significantly increased wheat and rice production in India.
